question_text: Two objects start moving at the same time. Object X travels at a constant speed of 6 m/s. Object Y accelerates uniformly from rest at 1.5 m/s². After how many seconds will both objects have travelled the same distance, and what will that distance be?
correct_answer: 8 seconds, distance = 48 metres. Using s = ut + 0.5at² for object Y: 6t = 0.5(1.5)t² gives 6t = 0.75t², so 6 = 0.75t (when t ≠ 0), thus t = 8 s. Distance = 6 × 8 = 48 m.
explanation: Set distances equal: for object X, s = 6t; for object Y, s = 0.5 × 1.5 × t². So 6t = 0.75t². Dividing by t (excluding t = 0): 6 = 0.75t, giving t = 8 seconds. Distance = 6 × 8 = 48 metres. At t = 0, both start at the same point; at t = 8 s, they meet again.
